ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S FOR ORDER THAT 1) FACTS IN FIRST SET OF REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ARE DEEMED ADMITTED 2) PROVIDE COMPLETE ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 2) PROVIDE COMPLETE ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 3) ETC.: DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS UNOPPOSED. HAVING REVIEWED THE MOVING PAPERS, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF IS DEEMED TO HAVE ADMITTED THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION THAT DEFENDANT SERVED ON JANUARY 28, 2013. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SERVED BY DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 28, 2013. PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED ANY OBJECTIONS TO THESE INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE, NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS TO THE DEMAND FOR INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS SERVED BY DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 28, 2013. PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED ANY OBJECTS TO THESE DOCUMENT REQUESTS. ALL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PROVIDED TO DEFENDANT NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. FINALLY, THE COURT AWARDS $450 IN SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF RELATED TO THE FAILURE TO TIMELY PROVIDE DISCOVERY RESPONSES. ORDER SIGNED. PRO TEM JUDGE: GEOFF HOWARD, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That 1)Facts In First Set Of Req. For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted 2) Provide Complete Answers To Form Interrogatories 2) Provide Complete Answers To Form Interrogatories 3) Etc
Off Calendar Jul-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Gregory B. Mouroux, AT&T, 415.778.1359, appearing for the Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S For Order That 1) Facts In First Set Of Request For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted 2) Provide Complete Answers To Form Interrogatories 2) Provide Complete Answers To Form Interrogatories 3) Etc.: Defendant's motion is unopposed. Having reviewed the moving papers, the court grants defendant's motion as follows: Plaintiff is deemed to have admitted the Requests for Admission that defendant served on January 28, 2013. Plaintiff is ordered to provide full and complete responses to the form interrogatories and special interrogatories served by defendant on January 28, 2013. Plaintiff has waived any objections to these interrogatories. Plaintiff is ordered to produce all responsive, non-privileged documents to the Demand for Inspection of Documents served by defendant on January 28, 2013. Plaintiff has waived any objects to these document requests. All discovery responses and documents shall be provided to defendant no later than 15 days after the date of this order. Finally, the Court awards $450 in sanctions against plaintiff related to the failure to timely provide discovery responses. Order signed. Pro Tem Judge: Geoff Howard, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
